outcome: Tree v1 created with 8 leaves; key addition q4-gear-ratio (Metzger 35x threshold). 6 public figures identified for future source reviews. 9 seed sources catalogued.
Metzger 2023 fetched + full extract written. Pass-01 research log captures sources, key insights, public figures for review. No claims yet (per protocol).

First-principles φ-threshold derivation. Tsiolkovsky math + Γ_X physics solid (Codex confirmed). Cost-parameter sweep collapses to ∞ thresholds — flagged in audit, deferred to reconcile. 5 claims added to claims.yaml.

Full leaf pass complete on q4-gear-ratio. 14 claims (10 high-conf, 4 medium). Codex tag-team caught real overclaims in calc and orphan numerical claims in write — both corrected per protocol. Leaf answer: ~35x threshold model-contingent, attainable per published TEAs (tent sublimation 10x headroom), LEO requires non-chemical architecture, TAI-grade automation compresses headroom further. Two flags for Avi: orphan claims minor cleanup pending, ξ time-evolution model needs rigorous treatment.

Initial scoping research. SatBase Feb 2026 source extracted (confirms 2026 Falcon 9 rideshare $7,000/kg and rising at $500/kg/year — contrary to the Starship-drives-prices-down thesis). 5 additional tier-2 sources catalogued for next pass. 6 public figures identified for source review (Musk, Patel, Handmer, Berger, McCalip, Citigroup).

First-principles Starship $/kg derivation. Three operational scenarios (optimistic 100-reuse $59-277/kg, partial 30-reuse $107-466/kg, pessimistic 10-reuse $194-878/kg). Codex audit caught 2 overclaims (partial-as-central, pessimistic-no-improvement) — both downgraded in response. Codex also surfaced 7 high-quality primary source URLs (FAA PEA, NASA TP, Shotwell statement, etc.) for reconcile pass.

Reconcile per Avi guidance: cite Musk $10/kg target + Citigroup $100/kg + $1600/kg-to-$100-150/kg cited projection, cross-check against calc scenarios. Three operational anchors: Musk (aspirational, outside envelope), industry-cited $100-150/kg (fits optimistic-late), B1076 34-reuse demonstration (validates partial scenario, 3x below optimistic). Codex audit caught 3 issues: zero-margin overclaim, possible booster-ID error, Falcon-9-to-Starship extrapolation caveat. All flagged in response. Codex bonus: surfaced primary Citigroup BusinessWire source for next pass.

Point-by-point source reviews written for 4 captured q1 sources: satbase-2026-falcon9, musk-10kg-target, shotwell-2017-refurb, b1076-34-reuses-2026. Each review uses verdict taxonomy (Consistent/Novel-supporting/Different-conclusion/Merits-investigation/Not-relevant) and cross-references reconciled framework from pass-03. Key novel-supporting finds: SatBase $500/kg/year structural increase contradicts naive Starship-drives-prices-down narrative; B1076 34-reuse milestone converts partial scenario from plausible to demonstrated. Codex audit deferred (4 reviews, would burn ~2min of Codex time each).

Tier-hierarchy research pass on q6 orbital demand. 27 extracts saved across S/A/B/C/D, plus 5 tier-E orientation. Tier S (7): Metzger 2023, Kornuta 2019, Crawford 2014, IDA D-13219, NASA OTPS SBSP 2024, NASA M2M Cargo 2024 + Logistics 2025. Tier A (2, below target): Marcy arXiv 2026, Mankins SPS-ALPHA NIAC. Tier B (5): Musk, Bezos, Metzger, Handmer, McCalip quote catalogues — bracket viability range 2x (Handmer) to 3.2x (McCalip). Tier C (9): Handmer x2, peraspera, luminix, introl, Cote, spaceambition, pickard, starcloud, NVIDIA, spacenews. Tier D (7): mainstream + industry press. Tier-A undercount flagged; primary PDFs (NASA OTPS, NASA M2M, Metzger, Kornuta) binary-fetch-failed, extracted via secondary aggregators with key numbers cross-validated. topics.yaml extended with 9 q6 topics.

Tier-hierarchy research pass on q5 capital buildup. 36 sources gathered: S=9 (Metzger 2013 bootstrap, Metzger 2023 economics, Jones 2020 breakeven, Sowers 2021 + 2018 CLPA, Metzger-Autry 2022 landing pads, Walther 2024 boulder construction, NASA FSP 2024 Glenn, Mueller 2022 construction planning); A=10 (Duchek 2024 350-kWth, AIAA 2025 mass-driver, Shubov 2021 GSFR, DARPA LunA-10, PwC 2026, CSIS Miller / MacDonald / Swope, IDA 2024, NASA OIG 2021 Artemis 93B); B=9 (Metzger, Sowers, Musk, Shotwell, Bezos, Zubrin, Handmer, Isaacman, Nelson — one card per figure); C=6 (SpaceNews + Payload Space LunA-10, Spaceflight Now 20B, Interesting Engineering + Space.com Musk, Breaking Defense CSIS); D=2 (ABC Bezos, CBS Isaacman). Tier-E orientation only. topics.yaml created with 15 q5 topics (q7 sibling added 11 more). PDF body extraction failed on several Tier-S sources (Jones 2020, Mueller 2022, IDA 2024, CLPA 2018, FSPS 2024 paywall); claims reconstructed from secondary coverage and flagged in leaf.yaml. Published anchors span ~3 orders of magnitude in capex (.5B Zubrin to >T Handmer) reflecting heterogeneous product class + transport + automation regimes. No claims yet — calc pass derives bottom-up.

Sources-sealed first-principles demand decomposition by use case (SDC, SBSP, depots, lunar cargo, Mars cargo, servicing) under three regimes (stall/BAU/TAI-C). 8 derived claims. Codex audit flagged 6 high-severity issues (SDC compute arithmetic, SDC solar arithmetic, stall scale text/calc mismatch, first-principles labeling, Earth-launch capacity comparison, lunar-sourced mass necessity overclaim). All accepted; calc revised. Key revised result: SDC dominates demand under BAU and TAI-C, but compute hardware (~50% of SDC mass) is Earth-launch-only — lunar manufacturing is necessary-but-not-sufficient for bulk mass half. TAI-C SDC ~2.7 Mt/yr exceeds Earth-launch supply by ~7x even under aggressive reusable-fleet assumptions.

Reconcile derived claims (q6.c1-c8) vs source extracts. Strong agreement on SDC ~40 t/MW (mine + peraspera 30-50 + luminix 42, three independent analyses). Strong agreement on Kornuta-Metzger 450 t/yr as lunar-derived sub-segment. Disagreements: McCalip 3.2x vs Handmer 2x cost multiple resolved by mapping to BAU vs TAI-C regimes; Cote bandwidth ceiling treated as ~5x sensitivity within TAI-C uncertainty; NASA OTPS LCOE skepticism vs BAU 5 GW SBSP resolved via strategic-deployment framing. 6 new factual claims added (q6.c9-c14) covering industry filings, public-figure cost-multiple range, Musk Mars cadence, Artemis cargo gap, OSAM market sizing, bandwidth ceiling. Codex audit: partially_defensible. Accepted: Cote OISL synthesis was unsupported by extracts; NASA OTPS BAU 5 GW under-supported (q6.c4 downgraded to medium confidence); q6.c6 conditionality on TAI-C scenario.
